content: Just as the linguistic meaning of reflexion as a learning practice in high school curricula frames intentional pause and critical review of one's own written output to refine communication, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this structured educational scaffolding to move beyond current perspective-contrast frameworks, building iterative reflective cycles modeled on guided student writing revision. This creates a new hybrid approach that ties explicit metacognitive learning routines from human education to automated LLM refinement.
